basic_info.name: Shimei Bay & Shenzhou Peninsula / 萬寧·石梅灣 與 神州半島
basic_info.location: Shimei Bay (石梅湾) and Shenzhou Peninsula (神州半岛, Dong'ao town area), Wanning City, Hainan Province; ~10-20 km north of Riyue Bay along Hainan east coast; ~100-110 km north of Sanya, ~3 hours south of Haikou; both bays accessed via Hainan East Ring Highway (G98) and high-speed Shenzhou Railway Station
basic_info.operator: Multi-resort destinations - no single surf operator; Shimei Bay anchored by Le Meridien Shimei Bay (开维 Le Meridien) and The Westin Shimei Bay Resort plus Aloft and Sheraton; Shenzhou Peninsula anchored by Grand Hyatt Shenzhou Peninsula Resort, Le Meridien Shenzhou Peninsula and Howard Johnson; surf services run by independent pro surf shops on each beach; broader Wanning surf cluster managed under Wanning Tourism Bureau as alternative breaks to flagship Riyue Bay
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural surf spots; both areas developed as resort/leisure destinations from late 2000s; surf scene secondary to Riyue Bay since ~2015)

technical_specs.pool_size: Shimei Bay: ~6 km arcing silver-sand beach with primary surf zone at adjacent Yangkou Bay (洋口湾) ~15 minutes west of main bay; Shenzhou Peninsula: ~10 km of east-facing sandy beaches surrounded by sea on three sides with multiple peak setups [uncertain exact lengths]
technical_specs.wave_height: Shimei Bay: seasonal average 0.4-1.2 m, gentle wave-kindergarten waters; Shenzhou Peninsula: 0.5-1.5 m typical, can build to 2 m in winter NE monsoon, summer 0.3-0.8 m; both lower-energy than adjacent Riyue Bay's point breaks
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell driven by NE monsoon Oct-Mar and occasional summer typhoon swells)
technical_specs.wave_types: Shimei Bay: predominantly mellow beach break over flat seafloor producing small, regular, beginner-friendly waves (locally nicknamed 'wave kindergarten' 浪花幼儿园); Shenzhou Peninsula: mix of beach break and minor point setups - sandy bottoms with consistent peelers; both bays NE-facing pick up the same NE monsoon swell as Riyue Bay but with smaller, less powerful waves
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 3-5 waves per set with 8-12 second intervals on NE monsoon days) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open ocean access; surf-school lessons typically 90-120 minutes when offered by mobile coaches from Riyue Bay)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (beach breaks offer both lefts and rights; no daily stance rotation needed)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Predominantly beach break over sand at both Shimei Bay and Shenzhou Peninsula; some minor point/cove setups on the peninsula's southern tip; secondary alternative breaks for advanced surfers when Riyue Bay is over-crowded or wind-blown out

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free open-ocean access at both bays; lessons less standardized than at Riyue/Houhai - typical 300-500 RMB for 2-hour group lesson; private lessons 500-800 RMB; resort-arranged surf experiences via Le Meridien/Westin/Grand Hyatt 600-1,200 RMB including transfer [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Limited tiered structure; mostly beginner group lessons (~300-400 RMB) at Shimei Bay's wave-kindergarten zone; intermediate/advanced surfers typically self-guide and seek the alternative spots when Riyue Bay is too crowded; resort surf packages combine lesson + photography [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top board ~100-200 RMB/day; resort hotels (Le Meridien Shimei Bay, Grand Hyatt Shenzhou) provide complimentary or paid surf board rental for guests; SUP and kayak rentals widely offered; rashguard typically included with lesson [uncertain pricing]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 300-500 RMB per person (2 hours); private 1-on-1 lesson 500-800 RMB; mobile coaches often travel from Riyue Bay's Tianxin Village cluster; resort-arranged sessions priced higher with concierge surcharge [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-day surf retreats less common than at Riyue Bay; resort + surf packages bundled by Le Meridien Shimei Bay and Grand Hyatt Shenzhou; photo/video add-on 200-500 RMB; multi-day intermediate camps 1,500-3,500 RMB through Riyue-Bay-based schools (GROM, Shaka) using these as overflow breaks [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: No formal bay-wide membership; resort hotel loyalty programs (Marriott Bonvoy at Le Meridien/Westin/Sheraton, World of Hyatt at Grand Hyatt Shenzhou) provide booking benefits; some Riyue-Bay-based clubs sell 10-session punch cards usable at multiple bays [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Resort properties (Westin, Le Meridien, Grand Hyatt) actively market beach team-building, MICE packages and corporate retreats; Shenzhou Peninsula hosts annual large-scale events (China Beach Ultimate Open with 200+ players Sep 20-21, 2025) demonstrating capacity; minimums 20+ people typical [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: October to April for surf (NE monsoon, ~80% surf days Nov-Feb); July-August occasional bigger swells for advanced surfers; year-round resort/beach holiday destination with average water 26C
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October-March: NE monsoon swells 0.5-2.0 m, period 7-12 s, offshore-cross winds; summer south-swell windows April-October produce smaller (~30% surf days) but cleaner days; typhoon swells Aug-Sep can deliver larger waves but often blown out
experience_guide.crowd_level: Significantly less crowded than Riyue Bay or Houhai year-round - both bays attract beach-resort tourists rather than dedicated surfers; Shenzhou Peninsula slightly busier due to Grand Hyatt and event venues; Shimei Bay especially uncrowded outside Le Meridien stretch; advanced surfers use these as overflow when Riyue Bay too crowded
experience_guide.booking_method: Surf lessons booked via resort concierge at Le Meridien Shimei Bay, Westin Shimei Bay, Grand Hyatt Shenzhou Peninsula, or directly through Riyue-Bay-based mobile coaches via WeChat; Trip.com/Ctrip and Klook list activity packages; walk-in surf rentals available on beach during peak season
experience_guide.booking_platform: Trip.com / Ctrip (携程), Marriott Bonvoy / Hyatt apps for resort packages, Dianping (大众点评), Meituan (美团), Xiaohongshu (小红书), WeChat mini-programs of mobile coaches, Klook
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash accepted; international credit cards accepted at Le Meridien, Westin, Sheraton, Aloft, Grand Hyatt and other 5-star resorts (key advantage over Riyue/Houhai for foreign visitors); small beach vendors WeChat/Alipay only
experience_guide.skill_required: Shimei Bay wave-kindergarten waters suit zero-base beginners; Shenzhou Peninsula suits beginner to intermediate; both serve advanced surfers seeking quieter alternative when Riyue Bay over-crowded; not the spot for true big-wave or expert tube hunting
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Resort-organized lessons typically accept ages 6+ with parental supervision; no formal upper age limit; participants must be able to swim; resort kids' clubs supervise younger children on beach activities
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 4/5 - both bays have shallow sandy entry, beach-resort amenities, kids' clubs at Grand Hyatt Shenzhou and Le Meridien Shimei; cleaner and quieter than Houhai; some debris on outer non-resort beach sections
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP and kayak rentals available at all major resorts and at the beach; Shenzhou Peninsula popular for SUP given consistent gentle conditions; resort water-sport huts also offer parasailing, jet ski, banana boat
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Resort photographers and independent freelancers offer beach/surf shoots ~300-800 RMB; surf-lesson packages may include GoPro footage; drone shoots arranged via concierge ~500-1,000 RMB [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: English support strong at all 5-star resorts (Le Meridien, Westin, Sheraton, Aloft, Grand Hyatt, Le Meridien Shenzhou); limited English at independent beach surf vendors; Mandarin dominant outside resort property
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: Resort properties (Westin, Grand Hyatt) provide accessible rooms and lobby ramps; beach access not formally certified; no adaptive surfing program documented [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking; beach breaks accommodate both stances
experience_guide.safety_notes: Generally safer than Riyue Bay's stronger point breaks; sandy bottoms, no significant reef hazards; lifeguards on resort-managed beaches during operating hours; rip currents can develop in winter NE monsoon; sun exposure intense year-round; resorts offer insured water-sport packages
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Shenzhou Peninsula hosts China Beach Ultimate Open (Sep 20-21, 2025) and various beach Xgames events; Shimei Bay used for music festivals, wedding events, and Sheraton/Westin MICE programs; not on WSL/ISA surf circuit (those go to Riyue Bay); occasional national surf-team training and qualifier events at peninsula's southern peaks [uncertain]

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Shimei Bay: The Westin Shimei Bay Resort (~700-1,500 RMB), Le Meridien Shimei Bay Beach Resort & Spa (~1,000-2,000 RMB; appeared in 'If You Are the One 2'), Sheraton Shimei Bay Resort, Aloft Shimei Bay; budget guesthouses in Huarun residential area 10-15 min walk to beach (~200-400 RMB); Shenzhou Peninsula: Grand Hyatt Shenzhou Peninsula Resort (~1,500-3,000 RMB), Le Meridien Shenzhou Peninsula Resort, Howard Johnson Resort Shenzhou Peninsula, Sheraton Shenzhou Peninsula
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: All surf-adjacent stays are integrated 5-star resort hotels (no surf-house lodge culture as at Riyue/Houhai); surf gear stored or rented through resort water-sports concierge; nearest surf-camp lodging is at Tianxin Village in Riyue Bay ~15-30 km away [uncertain pricing details]
surroundings.nearby_food: Resort restaurants (Westin Latitude, Le Meridien Latest Recipe, Grand Hyatt international and Cantonese restaurants); Shimei Bay 400 m commercial area with seafood, Sichuan, noodle and bakery options plus Phoenix Nine-Li Bookhouse cafe; Wanning specialties: Hele crab, Dongshan mutton, Houan chicken, fresh coconut; per-person spend 100-500 RMB at resorts, 50-150 RMB at village restaurants
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via Hainan East Ring Highway (G98); high-speed train to Shenzhou Station (35 min from main bay) or Wanning Station (more onward connections); resort shuttle services from rail stations (book in advance); Didi/taxi from local towns; electric bike rentals on Shenzhou Peninsula scenic road
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Shimei Bay: ~100 km / 1.5 hour drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); ~190 km / 2.5 hour drive from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); Shenzhou Peninsula: ~110 km / 2 hour drive from SYX, ~180 km / 2.5 hour from HAK; high-speed rail to Shenzhou Station ~35 min from Sanya, ~1 hour from Haikou
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Wanning surf-resort triangle: Riyue Bay (surf training base) + Shimei Bay (wave kindergarten + Le Meridien luxury) + Shenzhou Peninsula (Grand Hyatt + lighthouse + scenic coastal road); combine with Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden (兴隆热带植物园) and Dongshan Ridge (东山岭); typical 3-5 day Wanning resort + surf itinerary

pros: Significantly less crowded than Riyue Bay or Houhai - peaceful waves and beaches
Luxury 5-star resort infrastructure (Le Meridien, Westin, Grand Hyatt) with English support, international cards and amenities
Shimei Bay 'wave kindergarten' truly safe for absolute beginners and small children
Pristine 6 km silver sand beach (Shimei) and palm-lined scenic coastal road (Shenzhou Peninsula)
Easy alternative breaks for intermediate/advanced surfers when Riyue Bay is over-crowded or wind-blown
cons: Far fewer surf clubs and instructors than Riyue Bay or Houhai - lessons must be pre-arranged
Waves smaller and less powerful than Riyue Bay's flagship point breaks - not ideal for advanced wave hunters
No surf-village vibe - mostly resort guests and beach tourists rather than surf community
Some non-resort beach sections accumulate debris and lack lifeguard coverage
Higher overall cost when staying at flagship resorts (1,000-3,000 RMB/night)
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Trip.com / Tripadvisor: Shimei Bay 4.0-4.5/5 (rated 'Hainan's Most Beautiful Bay' by China National Geographic), Shenzhou Peninsula 4.0-4.5/5; Xiaohongshu features as quieter resort alternative to Sanya; Dianping reviews praise beach quality and hotel service, note distance from Sanya as drawback; surf-specific reviews label both as 'overflow' or 'kindergarten' breaks for beginners and intermediates seeking refuge from Riyue Bay crowds
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Beginner-to-intermediate surfers seeking quieter alternative to Riyue Bay; family resort holidays; couples on luxury beach escape; advanced surfers using as backup when Riyue Bay crowded or blown out; corporate retreat / MICE groups; international visitors needing English-speaking resort infrastructure
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Shimei Bay anchored by Marriott group (Le Meridien, Westin, Sheraton, Aloft); Shenzhou Peninsula anchored by Hyatt (Grand Hyatt) and Marriott (Le Meridien, Sheraton); both bays jointly promoted by Wanning Tourism Bureau as 'Wanning Surf Triangle' alongside Riyue Bay; surf services typically subcontracted to mobile coaches from Riyue Bay Tianxin Village cluster (GROM, Shaka, Hi Surf) [uncertain partnership details]
